text: He is no fool who gives what he cannot keep to gain what he cannot lose.
author: Jim Elliot

text: God is most glorified in us when we are most satisfied in him.
author: John Piper

text: Thou hast made us for thyself, O Lord, and our heart is restless until it finds its rest in thee.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: Prayer is not overcoming God's reluctance; it is laying hold of His highest willingness.
author: Richard Trench

text: Jesus Christ is not a cosmic errand boy. I mean no irreverence. It is simply that so many people have so contracted and so narrowed the function of Jesus Christ that He has become not the Lord of glory but a kind of celestial Santa Claus.
author: A.W. Tozer

text: The cross is the centre of the world's history; the incarnation of Christ and the crucifixion of our Lord are the pivot round which all the events of the ages revolve.
author: Alexander Maclaren

text: We are not necessarily doubting that God will do the best for us; we are wondering how painful the best will turn out to be.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Faith is deliberate confidence in the character of God whose ways you may not understand at the time.
author: Oswald Chambers

text: Preach the gospel to yourself every day.
author: Jerry Bridges

text: God does not love us because we are valuable. We are valuable because God loves us.
author: Martin Luther

text: The gospel is not a doctrine of the tongue, but of life. It cannot be grasped by reason and memory only, but it is fully understood when it possesses the whole soul and penetrates to the inner recesses of the heart.
author: John Calvin

text: Christ is no Moses, no exactor, no giver of laws, but a giver of grace, a Savior; He is infinite mercy and goodness, freely and bountifully given to us.
author: Martin Luther

text: The whole of life is a school of desire.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: Jesus does not give recipes that show the way to God as other teachers of religion do. He is himself the way.
author: Karl Barth

text: The Christian does not think God will love us because we are good, but that God will make us good because He loves us.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Joy is the serious business of heaven.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Justification means this: that the ungodly is declared righteous by God on account of Christ.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: God never uses anyone greatly until He tests them deeply.
author: A.W. Tozer

text: To fall in love with God is the greatest romance; to seek him the greatest adventure; to find him, the greatest human achievement.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: Let your desires be without measure, let your longings be ungoverned, and let all your petitions be unrestrained — for God is able to give you far more than you can desire.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: We are always in the forge or on the anvil; by trials God is shaping us for higher things.
author: Henry Ward Beecher

text: The man who kneels to God can stand up to anything.
author: Louis H. Evans

text: God loves each of us as if there were only one of us.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: The Gospel is not advice on how to live or tips for being better. It is news about what Christ has done.
author: Timothy Keller

text: God will either give us what we ask or give us what we would have asked if we knew everything He knows.
author: Timothy Keller
source: Prayer: Experiencing Awe and Intimacy with God

text: Sanctification is not about trying harder but about trusting more completely.
author: Dane Ortlund

text: Jesus is not a product to be marketed but a Savior to be proclaimed.
author: John Piper

text: His heart is the most tender and gentle of all hearts. It is toward you.
author: Dane Ortlund

text: Nothing in all creation is so like God as stillness.
author: Meister Eckhart

text: Our goal is not to be relevant to the culture but to be faithful to the King.
author: John Owen

text: It is not our hold of Christ that saves us, but Christ; it is not our joy in Christ that saves us, but Christ; it is not even faith in Christ, though that is the instrument; it is Christ's blood and merits.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: God is too good to be unkind and He is too wise to be mistaken. And when we cannot trace His hand, we must trust His heart.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: It is always safe to trust God. Oh, trust Him when you cannot trace Him.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: The enjoyment of God is the only happiness with which our souls can be satisfied.
author: Jonathan Edwards
source: The End for Which God Created the World

text: Grace is but glory begun, and glory is but grace perfected.
author: Jonathan Edwards

text: True religion consists, in great part, in holy affections.
author: Jonathan Edwards

text: God's grace is displayed most gloriously in the salvation of the unworthy.
author: Jonathan Edwards

text: Run, John, run, the law commands, but gives me neither legs nor hands; better news the gospel brings: it bids me fly and gives me wings.
author: John Bunyan
source: Grace Abounding to the Chief of Sinners

text: I have loved to hear my Lord spoken of; and wherever I have seen the print of his shoe in the earth, there have I coveted to set my foot too.
author: John Bunyan

text: What doth it profit thee to enter into deep discussion concerning the Holy Trinity, if thou lack humility?
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: Many words satisfy not the soul, but a good life refresheth the mind.
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: Better of a surety is a lowly peasant who serveth God, than a proud philosopher who watcheth the stars and neglecteth the knowledge of himself.
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: Rest from inordinate desire of knowledge, for therein is found much distraction and deceit.
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: Keep close to the written word; for when men leave the Scripture, they run into all extremes.
author: Richard Baxter

text: O study yourselves. You have a world of business within you.
author: Richard Baxter

text: The beginning of anxiety is the end of faith, and the beginning of true faith is the end of anxiety.
author: George Müller

text: The only way to learn strong faith is to endure great trials.
author: George Müller

text: The spirit of prayer is the fruit and token of the Spirit of adoption.
author: Andrew Murray
source: With Christ in the School of Prayer

text: Humility is simply the disposition which prepares the soul for living on trust.
author: Andrew Murray

text: There is a God-shaped vacuum in the heart of every man which cannot be filled by any created thing, but only by God the Creator, made known through Jesus Christ.
author: Blaise Pascal

text: In faith there is enough light for those who want to believe and enough shadows to blind those who don't.
author: Blaise Pascal

text: All of humanity's problems stem from man's inability to sit quietly in a room alone.
author: Blaise Pascal

text: You can give without loving, but you cannot love without giving.
author: Amy Carmichael

text: We profess to be strangers and pilgrims, seeking after a country of our own, yet we settle down in the most un-stranger-like fashion.
author: Amy Carmichael

text: The fact that I am a woman does not make me a different kind of Christian, but the fact that I am a Christian does make me a different kind of woman.
author: Elisabeth Elliot

text: God never withholds from His child that which His love and wisdom call good. God's refusals are always merciful — severe mercies at times, but mercies all the same.
author: Elisabeth Elliot

text: There is no pit so deep that God's love is not deeper still.
author: Corrie ten Boom

text: Worry does not empty tomorrow of its sorrow; it empties today of its strength.
author: Corrie ten Boom

text: A saint is not someone who never sins but someone who is grieved when he sins and who flees back to Christ for forgiveness.
author: D. Martyn Lloyd-Jones
source: Studies in the Sermon on the Mount

text: Revival is a period of unusual blessing and activity in the life of the Church.
author: D. Martyn Lloyd-Jones

text: Suffering is not the greatest evil; sin is. Suffering cannot separate us from God; sin can.
author: Thomas Watson

text: Prayer is the soul's breathing itself into the bosom of its heavenly Father.
author: Thomas Watson

text: The Word of God is the foundation upon which our faith is built, and the shield wherewith we must withstand all the assaults of our spiritual enemies.
author: Thomas Watson

text: Christianity is not a series of truths in the plural, but rather Truth spelled with a capital 'T'. Truth about total reality, not just religious things.
author: Francis Schaeffer

text: If there is no infinite personal God, then what we have is only the finite and impersonal, and there is no sufficient explanation for our existence.
author: Francis Schaeffer
source: He Is There and He Is Not Silent

text: Grace tried is better than grace, and more than grace; it is glory in its beginning.
author: Horatius Bonar

text: God does not love us less in winter than in summer. He does not love us less in our poverty than in our abundance.
author: Horatius Bonar

text: For every look at self, take ten looks at Christ.
author: Robert Murray M'Cheyne

text: A holy minister is an awful weapon in the hand of God.
author: Robert Murray M'Cheyne

text: Grace groweth best in winter.
author: Samuel Rutherford

text: Christ is more precious than anything that you can conceive. His love is better than life.
author: Samuel Rutherford

text: Those who are much employed in secret prayer are not easily drawn into open sin.
author: Thomas Brooks

text: Temptations and troubles are God's witnesses against our worldliness.
author: Thomas Brooks

text: Friendship is born at the moment when one person says to another: 'What! You too? I thought I was the only one.'
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Humility is not thinking less of yourself, it is thinking of yourself less.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: I believe in Christianity as I believe that the sun has risen; not only because I see it, but because by it I see everything else.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Resolved: to live with all my might, while I do live.
author: Jonathan Edwards

text: I would sooner breathe out my soul in prayer than do without it for a day.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: Visit many good books, but live in the Bible.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: This life therefore is not righteousness but growth in righteousness; not health but healing; not being but becoming; not rest but exercise.
author: Martin Luther
source: Defense and Explanation of All the Articles

text: Our Lord has written the promise of resurrection, not in books alone, but in every leaf in springtime.
author: Martin Luther

text: A dog barks when his master is attacked. I would be a coward if I saw that God's truth is attacked and yet would remain silent.
author: John Calvin

text: There is no worse screen to block out the Spirit than confidence in our own intelligence.
author: John Calvin

text: He that would be angry and sin not, must not be angry with anything but sin.
author: John Owen
source: The Grace and Duty of Being Spiritually Minded

text: What a man is in secret, in his closet, before his God, that he is and no more.
author: John Owen

text: Grant us, Lord, not to be anxious about earthly things, but to love things heavenly; and even now, while we are placed among things that are passing away, to cleave to those that shall abide.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: What comes into our minds when we think about God is the most important thing about us.
author: A.W. Tozer

text: The man who has God for his treasure has all things in One.
author: A.W. Tozer

text: The Lord's mercy often rides to the door of our heart on the black horse of affliction.
author: Charles Spurgeon

text: The way to heaven is ascending; we must be content to travel uphill, though it be hard and tiresome.
author: Jonathan Edwards

text: My sword I give to him that shall succeed me in my pilgrimage, and my courage and skill to him that can get it.
author: John Bunyan

text: There is naturally in every man a desire to know, but what profiteth knowledge without the fear of God?
author: Thomas à Kempis

text: The only power that can make you love God with all your heart is the Holy Spirit filling your heart with love.
author: Andrew Murray

text: I had rather wear out than rust out.
author: George Whitefield
source: Letters and Journals of George Whitefield

text: Sin promised Adam wisdom but made him a fool. It promised him he should be as a god, but made him like the devil.
author: Thomas Watson

text: The providence of God is like Hebrew words — it can only be read backwards.
author: John Flavel

text: I am not what I ought to be, I am not what I wish to be, I am not what I hope to be, but by the grace of God I am not what I was.
author: John Newton

text: God moves in a mysterious way, His wonders to perform; He plants His footsteps in the sea, and rides upon the storm.
author: William Cowper

text: Moses spent forty years thinking he was somebody, forty years learning he was nobody, and forty years discovering what God can do with a nobody.
author: D.L. Moody
source: Moody: His Words, Work, and Workers

text: Catch on fire with enthusiasm and people will come for miles to watch you burn.
author: John Wesley

text: I would as soon doubt God himself as doubt that he who began a good work in you will perfect it.
author: Samuel Rutherford

text: He who kneels most, stands best.
author: Horatius Bonar

text: If I could hear Christ praying for me in the next room, I would not fear a million enemies. Yet distance makes no difference. He is praying for me.
author: Robert Murray M'Cheyne

text: He that is weary, let him sit. My soul would stir and trade and roam; but hast thou less hands, than I have wits? There is no articling with thee.
author: George Herbert

text: There is more mercy in Christ than sin in us.
author: Richard Sibbes
